practice.name: Harvard-MIT Math Tournament #0i1j
practice.statement:
Problem:
A circle of radius $3$ crosses the center of a square of side length $2$. Find the difference between the areas of the nonoverlapping portions of the figures.

solutionReveal.markdown:
Solution:
Call the area of the square $s$, the area of the circle $c$, and the area of the overlapping portion $x$. The area of the circle not overlapped by the square is $c - x$ and the area of the square not overlapped by the circle is $s - x$, so the difference between these two is $(c - x) - (s - x) = c - s = 9\pi - 4$.
